creator: coolvibe@hackerheaven.org
text:
> make in kdebase2 fails to find X libs if /usr/X11R6/bin is not in the path.
> my root environment doesn't have X in the path.

Uh? /usr/X11R6/_bin_? No, you must be thinking of /usr/X11R6/lib. Did
you check /etc/ld.so.conf? what does $LD_LIBRARY_PATH say? I can build X
stuff fine from root in the ports and I'm using the standard tcsh
shell, but that shouldn't really matter, unless you got something funky
in your .*rc or profile/.profile files. I myself have no trouble using a
fairly standard STABLE and a CURRENT machine, which are frequently
cvsupped.

So check your shell startup scripts for LD_LIBRARY_PATH settings which
are borked or (as a last resort) enter the X11R6 lib path in
/etc/ld.so.conf and run a 'ldconfig' as root. If kdebase still moans
about missing includes, well, did you install the programmer packages
for X (Xprog.tgz I believe, not sure)? They contain needed header files.

Hope that helped a bit, Check your setup, there's nothing wrong with
kdelibs2 ('cuz I built 'em myself too, v2.2.2, no apparent problems and
KDE performs fine here.).

Cheers,
Emiel
-- 
Speak roughly to your little boy,
	And beat him when he sneezes:
He only does it to annoy
	Because he knows it teases.

	Wow!  wow!  wow!

I speak severely to my boy,
	And beat him when he sneezes:
For he can thoroughly enjoy
	The pepper when he pleases!

	Wow!  wow!  wow!
		-- Lewis Carroll, "Alice in Wonderland"
